Article.ArticleTitle: Impact of combined intradialytic exercise on quality of life, functional capacity, and dialysis adequacy in pediatric hemodialysis patients: A pilot study.

Abstract.AbstractText: Pediatric patients with chronic kidney disease (CKD) undergoing maintenance hemodialysis frequently exhibit compromised physical performance and reduced quality of life (QoL). The present study aimed to examine the effects of an eight-week combined intradialytic exercise intervention on QoL, functional capacity, and dialysis adequacy, defined as an index of urea clearance efficiency during hemodialysis, in this patient population.

In a quasi-experimental, one-group, before-and-after trial, five children (aged 8-18 years) with CKD on maintenance hemodialysis completed an eight-week intervention. The program, conducted three times a week during dialysis, consisted of upper-body resistance exercises (two sets of 10 repetitions at the 10-repetition maximum) and aerobic cycling (10-15 min, with intensity monitored via the Children's OMNI Scale). Outcomes included QoL (Pediatric Quality of Life Inventory [PedsQL™] 4.0, child and parent reports), functional capacity (handgrip strength and 60-s sit-to-stand [STS60] tests), and dialysis adequacy (single-pool Kt/V).

Child-reported PedsQL™ scores significantly improved for total QoL (Δx̄= 6.80, 95% CI [1.01, 12.58], p = 0.031, d = 1.46), emotional functioning (p = 0.043, d = 1.30), and social functioning (p = 0.042, d = 1.32). Handgrip strength (Δx̄= 2.20 kg, 95% CI [1.25, 3.14], p = 0.003, d = 2.90) and STS60 performance (Δx̄= 2.80 repetitions, 95% CI [0.57, 5.02], p = 0.025, d = 1.56) also increased significantly.

Combined intradialytic exercise significantly enhanced child-reported QoL and functional capacity in pediatric hemodialysis patients, particularly in emotional, social, and physical domains. These findings support the feasibility and efficacy of combined intradialytic exercise as an adjunct therapy for children with CKD.

This 8-week pilot study showed that combined intradialytic exercise upper-body resistance training (2 sets of 10 reps at 10RM) with aerobic cycling (10-15 min at an intensity of 3-5 on the Children's OMNI Scale), performed 3 times/week at the start of hemodialysis significantly improved child-reported quality of life, handgrip strength, and lower-body function.
